behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: local module wiring (HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue call suppression). The failure is at the boundary between verification and forwarding, not pure math alone. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is only about mismatch handling and non-forwarding; it does not require real OS/network/subprocess or model output, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Send an in-process HTTP request with a tampered body/signature pair to the handler, assert 401, and assert queue enqueue was not called (or no row written in local test queue store).

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: ordering across local components (handler response path and persistent queue write). This is not just one function; it is a cross-module sequencing guarantee. Higher levels rejected: behavior text requires persistence ordering but not real network/subprocess; a local real queue implementation (e.g., test DB/file) is sufficient, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler in-process with the real queue module backed by local test storage; instrument timestamps/hooks so test asserts queue write commit occurs before response completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: async handoff boundary between handler and downstream worker trigger. Real failure is incorrect awaiting/blocked promise across modules. Higher levels rejected: behavior text targets waiting semantics, not OS/network quirks; in-process delayed downstream stub exposes this, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor to block (e.g., unresolved promise / long delay), invoke handler, and assert response returns promptly while processing is still unfinished.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: real runtime timing/concurrency behavior (wall-clock SLA under concurrent load). This boundary is OS/event-loop scheduling and real server execution, which mocks/fake timers can hide. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML judgment and no multi-agent chain in text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual service process, generate concurrent prior slow events plus a new webhook, measure end-to-end wall-clock over real HTTP, and assert response latency <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: dedup state interaction between handler/idempotency logic and persistent queue/storage. Real failure appears when modules share persisted state, not in isolated pure logic only. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is dedup by ID, no requirement for real network/subprocess beyond local persistence, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same event ID twice through handler against local persistent test store; assert exactly one queued record and second request is handled without duplicate enqueue.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: module handoff/error mapping (queue full signal -> HTTP status/header). This is a boundary behavior between queue adapter and HTTP layer. Higher levels rejected: behavior text explicitly says 'when the queue reports it is full' (already signaled), so real OS/network/subprocess behavior is not required here; System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler with queue module/fake that returns a typed 'full' condition; assert HTTP 503 and correct Retry-After header in response.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type: queue component with real local persistence semantics and capacity accounting. A pure mocked queue would always pass and hide real enforcement bugs, so Unit is too low. Higher levels rejected: behavior is local capacity enforcement, with no required external network/subprocess; Integration with real local DB/filesystem backend is sufficient, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend in test mode with small capacity, enqueue until limit, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection/error state and no overflowed persisted records.
